name: GAF_SyteLine_ResourceGroupLookup_Tool_v1
description: Look up valid resource groups for work centers in SyteLine

data.api_docs:
Method: GET
Endpoint: /load/SLWcResourceGroups
CRITICAL: The endpoint above is RELATIVE — the service path 'CSI/IDORequestService/ido' is prepended automatically. Do NOT repeat the service path in the endpoint or add tenant names, config values, 'api', 'invoke', or extra segments.
Headers:
X-Infor-MongooseConfig: <site>
Query Parameters:
Parameter: properties
Format: STRING
Required: True
Description: CRITICAL: ONLY use these exact properties: Wc,Rgid,RgrpSLTYPE,QtyResources. Do NOT request any other properties.
Parameter: filter
Format: STRING
Required: True
Description: SQL WHERE clause to filter by work center codes. Use IN clause for multiple WCs. Example: Wc IN ('FA-400','INS-20','PG-300','CUT-10')
Parameter: orderby
Format: STRING
Required: False
Description: Sort order. Use: Wc ASC
Parameter: recordCap
Format: INTEGER
Required: False
Description: Maximum records to return. Set to at least 2x the number of WCs (some WCs have multiple resource groups).
Response Fields:
- Wc: Work center code (e.g., FA-400)
- Rgid: Resource group ID — the EXACT valid value to use in ResourceGroupInsert (e.g., FA-400-LRG, PG-300-MRG)
- RgrpSLTYPE: Resource group type. L=Labor, M=Machine, O=Other
- QtyResources: Number of resources in the group
IMPORTANT: Use these EXACT Rgid values when calling ResourceGroupInsert. Do NOT guess or derive Rgid from naming convention.
RgrpSLTYPE determines SchedDrv alignment: L -> SchedDrv='L', M -> SchedDrv='M'.
Example Request:
Look up resource groups for 3 work centers:
GET /load/SLWcResourceGroups?properties=Wc,Rgid,RgrpSLTYPE,QtyResources&filter=Wc IN ('FA-400','INS-20','PG-300')&orderby=Wc ASC&recordCap=10
data.responseInstructions:
The API responds with a JSON object containing an Items array.
Key Response Fields:
- Items: Array of resource group mappings (empty if no WCs match)
- Each item contains: Wc, Rgid, RgrpSLTYPE, QtyResources
Parse the response to build a WC-to-Rgid mapping:
For each item, record: Wc -> Rgid (exact value), RgrpSLTYPE (L or M)
If a WC has multiple resource groups (e.g., both LRG and MRG), prefer:
- LRG (Labor, RgrpSLTYPE='L') for assembly, inspection, staging, packaging operations
- MRG (Machine, RgrpSLTYPE='M') for machining, cutting, drilling, grinding, molding operations
If a WC from the filter has NO matching records, that WC has no valid resource group — report this as an error.
CRITICAL - IF ERROR OCCURS INCLUDE THE BELOW IN RESPONSE
  ---DEBUG---
  FULL_URL: [The complete URL that was called]
  FULL_RESPONSE: [The complete API response received]
  ---END DEBUG---

instructions:
Look up ACTUAL valid resource groups for work centers from SLWcResourceGroups.
Returns exact Rgid, type (L=Labor/M=Machine), and QtyResources per WC.
ONLY request properties: Wc,Rgid,RgrpSLTYPE,QtyResources.
Use IN clause to query multiple WCs in one call.

SITE: <site> placeholder = [TENANT]_DALS from agent input.
